stem: A program asks the user to enter exam scores. The user types `done` (in any case) when they have finished. The program then prints the total of the scores.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Accept OCR pseudocode or Python. Look for: sentinel detection (case-insensitive 'done'), running total, defensive handling for non-numeric input, final output. Marks scale with how robust the loop is.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Write a program that lets the user enter as many scores as they want and types 'done' (any case) to finish. The program then prints the total of the entered scores.

mark_points.text: loops, asking for input each time
detects the sentinel 'done' case-insensitively and exits the loop
accumulates a running total of the numeric scores
prints the final total once the loop ends

misconceptions.label: Hard-coded count
misconceptions.description: A `for i in range(N)` loop is not a sentinel-controlled loop — the user controls when to stop in this scenario.
